---
title: An Important Talk
subtitle: About things
format:
  aog-present-revealjs: default
author:
  - name: Aaron Gullickson
    orcid: 0000-0001-7237-8131
    email: aarong@uoregon.edu
    url: https://aarongullickson.netlify.app/
    attributes:
      corresponding: true
    affiliations:
      - id: uo
        name: University of Oregon
        department: Sociology
        address: 727 PLC
        city: Eugene
        region: OR
        country: USA
        postal-code: 97405
#date: last-modified
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(texreg)
library(gt)
library(palmerpenguins)
```

```{r}
theme_myslides <- theme_bw()+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(size=24, hjust=0),
        plot.title.position = "panel",
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size=16, hjust=0),
        plot.caption = element_text(size=14, hjust=1, vjust=1),
        plot.caption.position = "panel",
        text = element_text(colour="#000000"),
        axis.title = element_text(size=20),
        axis.line = element_line(colour="#000000", size = 1),
        axis.ticks = element_line(colour="#000000", size = 1),
        axis.text = element_text(size=16),
        legend.title = element_text(size=20),
        legend.text = element_text(size=16),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(), 
        panel.border = element_rect(fill=NA, colour=NA),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "#EAEBEE",colour = NA),
        plot.background = element_rect(fill = "#EAEBEE",colour = NA),
        legend.background = element_rect(fill = "#EAEBEE",colour = NA),
        legend.key = element_rect(fill = "#EAEBEE",colour = NA), 
        legend.position = "right")
theme_set(theme_myslides)
```

# An Inverse Cover Slide {background-color="#18273F" background-image="images/mikael-kristenson-3aVlWP-7bg8-unsplash.jpg"}

## Basic Bullet Points

- A really [important](https://google.com) point
- This is also `important`
- But don't forget this other thing
  - Although on the other hand, one should consider this

::: {.notes}
There are also some notes here, if you know how to find them.
:::

## Two Columns

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="40%"}
### Column 1

This column is taking up 40% of the width and the other column is taking up 60% of the width.
:::
::: {.column width="60%"}
### Column 2
This is accomplished with divs:
```markdown
:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="40%"}
### Column 1 stuff
:::
::: {.column width="60%"}
:::
### Column 2 stuff
::::
```
:::

::::

## Using Fragments

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="40%"}
I can use fragments to display arbitrary content incrementally.

```markdown
::: {.fragment}
Some Content
:::
```
I wonder what content is in the other column?
:::
::: {.column width="60%"}
::: {.fragment}
![Its a Sloth!](images/sloth1.png)
:::
:::

::::


## Tables Work Well {.smaller}

```{r}
#| label: tbl-addhealth
#| tbl-cap: Add Health summary statistics by grade
#| eval: false
popularity |>
  group_by(grade) |>
  summarize(n=n(), m_nom=mean(indegree),  p_drink=mean(alcoholuse=="Drinker"),
            p_smoke=mean(smoker=="Smoker")) |>
  gt() |>
  fmt_integer(1:2) |>
  fmt_number(3, decimals=2) |>
  fmt_percent(4:5, decimals = 1) |>
  cols_label(grade="Grade", n="N", m_nom="Mean friend nominations",
             p_drink="Drink Regularly", p_smoke="Smoke Regularly") |>
  tab_spanner("Percent substance use", 4:5)
```

This table was produced directly in `gt`. I also fit more on this slide by specifying:

```markdown
## Slide Title {.smaller}
```

## Figures Also Work Well

```{r}
#| label: fig-dist
#| fig-cap: Flipper length and body mass for Adelie, Chinstrap and Gentoo Penguins
ggplot(data = penguins, aes(x = flipper_length_mm, y = body_mass_g)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = species, shape = species), size = 3, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("darkorange","purple","cyan4")) +
  labs(x = "Flipper length (mm)",
       y = "Body mass (g)",
       color = "Penguin species",
       shape = "Penguin species")
```
